# UE Python API ‚Äî Engine Module

### `unreal.AnimNotifyEventReference`
Inherits: `StructBase` | Header: `AnimNotifyQueue.h`

Anim Notify Event Reference

**Methods** (6):
  - `get_current_animation_notify_state_time()` -> `float` ‚Äî Gets the current time in seconds relative to the start of the notify state, clamped to the range of the notify state
  - `get_current_animation_notify_state_time_ratio()` -> `float` ‚Äî Gets the current time as a ratio (0 -> 1) relative to the start of the notify state
  - `get_current_animation_time()` -> `float` ‚Äî Get the current anim notify time in seconds for when this notify was fired
  - `get_current_animation_time_ratio()` -> `float` ‚Äî Get the current anim notify time as a ratio (0 -> 1) through the animation for when this notify was fired
  - `is_blending_out()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Gets whether this notify comes from a source that is blending out.
  - `notify_state_reached_end()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Get whether the notify state reached the end (was not cancelled)

Inherits: `Object` | Header: `Actor.h`

  - `add_actor_local_rotation(delta_rotation, sweep, teleport)` -> `HitResult` ‚Äî Adds a delta to the rotation of this component in its local reference frame
  - `add_actor_local_transform(new_transform, sweep, teleport)` -> `HitResult` ‚Äî Adds a delta to the transform of this component in its local reference frame
  - `add_actor_world_offset(delta_location, sweep, teleport)` -> `HitResult` ‚Äî Adds a delta to the location of this actor in world space.
  - `add_actor_world_rotation(delta_rotation, sweep, teleport)` -> `HitResult` ‚Äî Adds a delta to the rotation of this actor in world space.
  - `add_actor_world_transform(delta_transform, sweep, teleport)` -> `HitResult` ‚Äî Adds a delta to the transform of this actor in world space. Ignores scale and sets it to (1,1,1).
  - `add_actor_world_transform_keep_scale(delta_transform, sweep, teleport)` -> `HitResult` ‚Äî Adds a delta to the transform of this actor in world space. Scale is unchanged.

  - `attach_to_component(parent, socket_name, location_rule, rotation_rule, scale_rule, weld_simulated_bodies = True)` -> `bool` ‚Äî Attaches the RootComponent of this Actor to the supplied component, optionally at a named socket. It is not valid to cal...

  - `get_actor_forward_vector()` -> `Vector` ‚Äî Get the forward (X) vector (length 1.0) from this Actor, in world space.

### `unreal.ActorComponent`
Inherits: `Object` | Header: `ActorComponent.h`

ActorComponent is the base class for components that define reusable behavior that can be added to different types of Actors. ActorComponents that have a transform are known as SceneComponents and tho...

### `unreal.AudioBus`
Inherits: `Object` | Header: `AudioBus.h`

An audio bus is an object which represents an audio patch cord. Audio can be sent to it. It can be sonified using USoundSourceBuses. Instances of the audio bus are created in the audio engine.

### `unreal.CheatManager`
Inherits: `Object` | Header: `CheatManager.h`

Cheat Manager is a central blueprint to implement test and debug code and actions that are not to ship with the game. As the Cheat Manager is not instanced in shipping builds, it is for debugging purp...

**Methods** (16):
  - `change_size(f)` -> `None` ‚Äî Scale the player√¢¬Ä¬ôs size to be F * default size.
  - `damage_target(damage_amount)` -> `None` ‚Äî Damage the actor you√¢¬Ä¬ôre looking at (sourced from the player).
  - `destroy_target()` -> `None` ‚Äî Destroy the actor you√¢¬Ä¬ôre looking at.
  - `disable_debug_camera()` -> `None` ‚Äî Switch controller from debug camera back to normal controller
  - `enable_debug_camera()` -> `None` ‚Äî Switch controller to debug camera without locking gameplay and with locking local player controller input
  - `fly()` -> `None` ‚Äî Pawn can fly.
  - `freeze_frame(delay)` -> `None` ‚Äî Pause the game for Delay seconds.
  - `get_player_controller()` -> `PlayerController` ‚Äî Get Player Controller
  - `ghost()` -> `None` ‚Äî Pawn no longer collides with the world, and can fly
  - `god()` -> `None` ‚Äî Invulnerability cheat.
  - `players_only()` -> `None` ‚Äî Freeze everything in the level except for players.
  - `receive_end_play()` -> `None` ‚Äî This is the End Play event for the CheatManager
  - `receive_init_cheat_manager()` -> `None` ‚Äî BP implementable event for when CheatManager is created to allow any needed initialization.
  - `slomo(new_time_dilation)` -> `None` ‚Äî Modify time dilation to change apparent speed of passage of time. e.g. √¢¬Ä¬úSlomo 0.1√¢¬Ä¬ù makes everything move very slowly...
  - `teleport()` -> `None` ‚Äî Teleport to surface player is looking at.
  - `walk()` -> `None` ‚Äî Return to walking movement mode from Fly or Ghost cheat.

**Properties** (1):
  - `debug_camera_controller_class`: `Class` [Read-Only] ‚Äî [Read-Only] Debug camera - used to have independent camera without stopping gameplay (type(Class))

### `unreal.CheatManagerExtension`
Inherits: `Object` | Header: `CheatManager.h`

A cheat manager extension can extend the main cheat manager in a modular way, being enabled or disabled when the system associated with the cheats is enabled or disabled

  - `added_to_cheat_manager()` -> `None` ‚Äî Added to Cheat Manager
  - `get_player_controller()` -> `PlayerController` ‚Äî Get Player Controller
  - `removed_from_cheat_manager()` -> `None` ‚Äî Removed from Cheat Manager

### `unreal.GameSession`
Inherits: `Info` | Header: `GameSession.h`

Acts as a game-specific wrapper around the session interface. The game code makes calls to this when it needs to interact with the session interface. A game session exists only the server, while runni...

### `unreal.GameState`
Inherits: `GameStateBase` | Header: `GameState.h`

GameState is a subclass of GameStateBase that behaves like a multiplayer match-based game. It is tied to functionality in GameMode.

**Properties** (3):
  - `elapsed_time`: `int` [Read-Only] ‚Äî [Read-Only] Elapsed game time since match has started. (int32)
  - `match_state`: `Name` [Read-Only] ‚Äî [Read-Only] What match state we are currently in (Name)
  - `previous_match_state`: `Name` [Read-Only] ‚Äî [Read-Only] Previous map state, used to handle if multiple transitions happen per frame (Name)

### `unreal.GameStateBase`
Inherits: `Info` | Header: `GameStateBase.h`

GameStateBase is a class that manages the game√¢¬Ä¬ôs global state, and is spawned by GameModeBase. It exists on both the client and the server and is fully replicated.

**Methods** (6):
  - `get_player_respawn_delay(controller)` -> `float` ‚Äî Returns how much time needs to be spent before a player can respawn
  - `get_player_start_time(controller)` -> `float` ‚Äî Returns the time that should be used as when a player started
  - `get_server_world_time_seconds()` -> `double` ‚Äî Returns the simulated TimeSeconds on the server, will be synchronized on client and server
  - `has_begun_play()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Returns true if the world has started play (called BeginPlay on actors)
  - `has_match_ended()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Returns true if the match can be considered ended. Defaults to false.
  - `has_match_started()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Returns true if the world has started match (called MatchStarted callbacks)

**Properties** (4):
  - `authority_game_mode`: `GameModeBase` [Read-Only] ‚Äî [Read-Only] Instance of the current game mode, exists only on the server. For non-authority clients,...
  - `game_mode_class`: `Class` [Read-Only] ‚Äî [Read-Only] Class of the server√¢¬Ä¬ôs game mode, assigned by GameModeBase. (type(Class))
  - `player_array`: `None` [Read-Only] ‚Äî [Read-Only] Array of all PlayerStates, maintained on both server and clients (PlayerStates are alway...
  - `spectator_class`: `Class` [Read-Only] ‚Äî [Read-Only] Class used by spectators, assigned by GameModeBase. (type(Class))

### `unreal.GameUserSettings`
Inherits: `Object` | Header: `GameUserSettings.h`

Stores user settings for a game (for example graphics and sound settings), with the ability to save and load to and from a file.

**Methods** (72):
  - `apply_hardware_benchmark_results()` -> `None` ‚Äî Applies the settings stored in ScalabilityQuality and saves settings
  - `apply_non_resolution_settings()` -> `None` ‚Äî Apply Non Resolution Settings
  - `apply_resolution_settings(check_for_command_line_overrides)` -> `None` ‚Äî Apply Resolution Settings
  - `apply_settings(check_for_command_line_overrides = True)` -> `None` ‚Äî Applies all current user settings to the game and saves to permanent storage (e.g. file), optionally checking for comman...
  - `confirm_video_mode()` -> `None` ‚Äî Mark current video mode settings (fullscreenmode/resolution) as being confirmed by the user
  - `enable_hdr_display_output(enable, display_nits = 1000)` -> `None` ‚Äî Enables or disables HDR display output. Can be called again to change the desired nit level
  - `get_anti_aliasing_quality()` -> `int32` ‚Äî Returns the anti-aliasing quality (0..4, higher is better)
  - `get_audio_quality_level()` -> `int32` ‚Äî Returns the user√¢¬Ä¬ôs audio quality level setting
  - `get_current_hdr_display_nits()` -> `int32` ‚Äî Returns 0 if HDR isn√¢¬Ä¬ôt supported or is turned off
  - `get_default_resolution()` -> `IntPoint` [classmethod] ‚Äî Returns the default resolution when no resolution is set
  - `get_default_resolution_scale()` -> `float` ‚Äî Gets the desired resolution quality based on DesiredScreenWidth/Height and the current screen resolution
  - `get_default_window_mode()` -> `WindowMode` [classmethod] ‚Äî Returns the default window mode when no mode is set
  - `get_default_window_position()` -> `IntPoint` [classmethod] ‚Äî Returns the default window position when no position is set
  - `get_desktop_resolution()` -> `IntPoint` ‚Äî Returns user√¢¬Ä¬ôs desktop resolution, in pixels.
  - `get_foliage_quality()` -> `int32` ‚Äî Returns the foliage quality (0..4, higher is better)
  - `get_frame_pace()` -> `int32` [classmethod] ‚Äî Gets the current frame pacing frame rate in fps, or 0 if none
  - `get_frame_rate_limit()` -> `float` ‚Äî Gets the user√¢¬Ä¬ôs frame rate limit (0 indiciates the frame rate limit is disabled)
  - `get_fullscreen_mode()` -> `WindowMode` ‚Äî Returns the user setting for game window fullscreen mode.

### `unreal.MaterialInterface`
Inherits: `Object` | Header: `MaterialInterface.h`

Material Interface

**Methods** (11):
  - `add_asset_user_data_of_class(user_data_class)` -> `bool` ‚Äî Creates and adds an instance of the provided AssetUserData class to the target asset.
  - `get_asset_user_data_of_class(user_data_class)` -> `AssetUserData` ‚Äî Returns an instance of the provided AssetUserData class if it√¢¬Ä¬ôs contained in the target asset.
  - `get_base_material()` -> `Material` ‚Äî Walks up parent chain and finds the base Material that this is an instance of. Just calls the virtual GetMaterial()
  - `get_blend_mode()` -> `BlendMode` ‚Äî Get Blend Mode
  - `get_nanite_overide_material()` -> `MaterialInterface` ‚Äî Get the associated nanite override material.
  - `get_parameter_info(association, parameter_name, layer_function)` -> `MaterialParameterInfo` ‚Äî Get Parameter Info
  - `get_physical_material()` -> `PhysicalMaterial` ‚Äî Return a pointer to the physical material used by this material instance.

  - `get_physical_material_mask()` -> `PhysicalMaterialMask` ‚Äî Return a pointer to the physical material mask used by this material instance.

### `unreal.MaterialParameterCollection`
Inherits: `Object` | Header: `MaterialParameterCollection.h`

Asset class that contains a list of parameter names and their default values. Any number of materials can reference these parameters and get new values when the parameter values are changed.

**Methods** (4):
  - `get_scalar_parameter_default_value(parameter_found=bool)` ‚Äî Gets the default value of a scalar parameter from a material collection.
  - `get_scalar_parameter_names()` -> `Array [ Name ]` ‚Äî Returns an array of the names of all the scalar parameters in this Material Parameter Collection *
  - `get_vector_parameter_default_value(parameter_found=bool)` ‚Äî Gets the default value of a scalar parameter from a material collection.
  - `get_vector_parameter_names()` -> `Array [ Name ]` ‚Äî Returns an array of the names of all the vector parameters in this Material Parameter Collection *

### `unreal.NavMovementComponent`
Inherits: `MovementComponent` | Header: `NavMovementComponent.h`

NavMovementComponent defines base functionality for MovementComponents that move any √¢¬Ä¬òagent√¢¬Ä¬ô that may be involved in AI pathfinding.

**Methods** (11):
  - `get_max_speed_for_nav_movement()` -> `float` ‚Äî Get maximum movement speed of the agent
  - `get_velocity_for_nav_movement()` -> `Vector` ‚Äî Get the current velocity of the movement component
  - `is_crouching()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Returns true if currently crouching
  - `is_falling()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Returns true if currently falling (not flying, in a non-fluid volume, and not on the ground)
  - `is_flying()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Returns true if currently flying (moving through a non-fluid volume without resting on the ground)
  - `is_moving_on_ground()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Returns true if currently moving on the ground (e.g. walking or driving)
  - `is_swimming()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Returns true if currently swimming (moving through a fluid volume)
  - `request_direct_move(move_velocity, force_max_speed)` -> `None` ‚Äî path following: request movement through a velocity directly
  - `request_path_move(move_input)` -> `None` ‚Äî path following: request movement through a new move input (normal vector = full strength)
  - `stop_active_movement()` -> `None` ‚Äî Stops applying further movement (usually zeros acceleration).
  - `stop_movement_keep_pathing()` -> `None` ‚Äî Stops movement immediately (reset velocity) but keeps following current path

### `unreal.PlayerController`
Inherits: `Controller` | Header: `PlayerController.h`

PlayerControllers are used by human players to control Pawns.

  - `get_delayed_timecode()` -> `Timecode` ‚Äî Return the delayed frame time converted into a timecode value.
  - `get_frame_rate()` -> `FrameRate` ‚Äî Return the frame rate of the frame time.
  - `get_qualified_frame_time()` -> `QualifiedTime` ‚Äî Return current frame time. Since it may be called several times per frame, it is suggested to return a cached value.

### `unreal.TimelineComponent`
Inherits: `ActorComponent` | Header: `TimelineComponent.h`

TimelineComponent holds a series of events, floats, vectors or colors with associated keyframes. Events can be triggered at keyframes along the timeline. Floats, vectors, and colors are interpolated b...

**Methods** (29):
  - `add_event(time, event_func)` -> `None` ‚Äî Add a callback event to the timeline
  - `add_interp_float(float_curve, interp_func, property_name = 'None', track_name = 'None')` -> `None` ‚Äî Add a float interpolation to the timeline
  - `add_interp_linear_color(linear_color_curve, interp_func, property_name = 'None', track_name = 'None')` -> `None` ‚Äî Add a linear color interpolation to the timeline
  - `add_interp_vector(vector_curve, interp_func, property_name = 'None', track_name = 'None')` -> `None` ‚Äî Add a vector interpolation to the timeline
  - `get_ignore_time_dilation()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Get whether to ignore time dilation.
  - `get_play_rate()` -> `float` ‚Äî Get the current play rate for this timeline
  - `get_playback_position()` -> `float` ‚Äî Get the current playback position of the Timeline
  - `get_scaled_timeline_length()` -> `float` ‚Äî Get length of the timeline divided by the play rate
  - `get_timeline_length()` -> `float` ‚Äî Get length of the timeline
  - `is_looping()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Get whether we are looping or not
  - `is_playing()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Get whether this timeline is playing or not.
  - `is_reversing()` -> `bool` ‚Äî Get whether we are reversing or not
  - `play()` -> `None` ‚Äî Start playback of timeline
  - `play_from_start()` -> `None` ‚Äî Start playback of timeline from the start
  - `reverse()` -> `None` ‚Äî Start playback of timeline in reverse
  - `reverse_from_end()` -> `None` ‚Äî Start playback of timeline in reverse from the end
  - `set_float_curve(new_float_curve, float_track_name)` -> `None` ‚Äî Update a certain float track√¢¬Ä¬ôs curve
  - `set_ignore_time_dilation(new_ignore_time_dilation)` -> `None` ‚Äî Set whether to ignore time dilation.
  - `set_linear_color_curve(new_linear_color_curve, linear_color_track_name)` -> `None` ‚Äî Update a certain linear color track√¢¬Ä¬ôs curve
  - `set_looping(new_looping)` -> `None` ‚Äî true means we would loop, false means we should not.
  - `set_new_time(new_time)` -> `None` ‚Äî Set the new playback position time to use
  - `set_play_rate(new_rate)` -> `None` ‚Äî Sets the new play rate for this timeline
  - `set_playback_position(new_position, fire_events, fire_update = True)` -> `None` ‚Äî Jump to a position in the timeline.
  - `set_timeline_finished_func(new_timeline_finished_func)` -> `None` ‚Äî Set the delegate to call when timeline is finished
  - `set_timeline_length(new_length)` -> `None` ‚Äî Set length of the timeline
  - `set_timeline_length_mode(new_length_mode)` -> `None` ‚Äî Sets the length mode of the timeline
  - `set_timeline_post_update_func(new_timeline_post_update_func)` -> `None` ‚Äî Set the delegate to call after each timeline tick
  - `set_vector_curve(new_vector_curve, vector_track_name)` -> `None` ‚Äî Update a certain vector track√¢¬Ä¬ôs curve
  - `stop()` -> `None` ‚Äî Stop playback of timeline

  - `EXPO_OUT`: `EasingFunc = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Easing out using an exponential 9

### `unreal.HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures`
Inherits: `EnumBase` | Header: `InputSettings.h`

A bitmask of supported features that a hardware device has.

**Properties** (21):
  - `ACCELERATION`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This device can track acceleration in the users physical space 1024
  - `AUDIO_BASED_VIBRATIONS`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Does this device support vibrations sourced from an audio file? 512
  - `CAMERA`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Does this device have a camera on it that we can access? 16
  - `CUSTOM_A`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Some custom flags that can be used in your game if you have custom hardware! 16777216
  - `CUSTOM_B`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî 33554432
  - `CUSTOM_C`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî 67108864
  - `CUSTOM_D`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî 134217728
  - `DRUMS`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This device has the capabilities of drums (symbols, foot pedal, etc) 32768
  - `FORCE_FEEDBACK`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Flagged true if this device supports force feedback 256
  - `GAMEPAD`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This device has basic gamepad support 4
  - `GUITAR`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This device has the capabilities of a guitar (whammy bar, tilt, etc) 16384
  - `KEYPRESS`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This device can support basic key presses 1
  - `LIGHTS`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This hardware supports setting a light color (such as an LED light bar) 64
  - `MICROPHONE`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This device has a microphone on it that you can get audio from 4096
  - `MOTION_TRACKING`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Can this device track motion in a 3D space? (VR controllers, headset, etc) 32
  - `ORIENTATION`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This device can track the orientation in world space, such as a gyroscope 8192
  - `POINTER`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This device can handle basic pointer behavior, such as a mouse 2
  - `TOUCH`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This device supports touch in some capactiy (tablet, controller with a touch pad, etc) 8
  - `TRIGGER_HAPTICS`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Does this device have trigger haptics available? 128
  - `UNSPECIFIED`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî A device that has not specified the type 0
  - `VIRTUAL`: `HardwareDeviceSupportedFeatures = Ellipsis` ‚Äî This is a virtual device simulating input, not a physical device 2048

  - `TSE_LINEAR`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî The source encoding is considered linear (before optional sRGB encoding is applied). 1
  - `TSE_NONE`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî The source encoding is not overridden. 0

  - `TSE_RED_LOG`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî RED Log source encoding to be linearized (before optional sRGB encoding is applied). 8
  - `TSE_RED_LOG3G10`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî RED Log3G10 source encoding to be linearized (before optional sRGB encoding is applied). 9
  - `TSE_ST2084`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî SMPTE ST 2084/PQ source encoding to be linearized (before optional sRGB encoding is applied). 3
  - `TSE_S_LOG1`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Sony SLog1 source encoding to be linearized (before optional sRGB encoding is applied). 10
  - `TSE_S_LOG2`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Sony SLog2 source encoding to be linearized (before optional sRGB encoding is applied). 11
  - `TSE_S_LOG3`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Sony SLog3 source encoding to be linearized (before optional sRGB encoding is applied). 12
  - `TSE_S_RGB`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî sRGB source encoding to be linearized (before optional sRGB encoding is applied). 2
  - `TSE_V_LOG`: `TextureSourceEncoding = Ellipsis` ‚Äî Panasonic V-Log source encoding to be linearized (before optional sRGB encoding is applied). 16
